futureCollectionCommandCandidates.intendedUse: Collect repository-relative changed paths for a committed or explicit ref range.
Collect change status plus rename/deletion information for a later richer checker input.
Potential later mode for untracked and working-tree state visibility.

baseRefPolicy.reason: A committed or explicit ref range is more reproducible than an implicit working-tree diff.
unresolved: whether HEAD~1..HEAD is acceptable outside single-commit previews

headRefPolicy.reason: The checker should know which revision produced the candidate changed-file list.
unresolved: how detached HEAD and local branches are represented

workingTreePolicy.reason: Working-tree diffs are useful for local development, but they are less reproducible and require explicit dirty-state semantics.

stagedChangesPolicy.reason: Staged-only changes should not be silently mixed with committed-range checking.

untrackedFilesPolicy.reason: Untracked files are not represented by basic committed-range diff commands and need a separate authority and path policy.

renamedFilesPolicy.reason: A name-only first slice can collect path presence, but a later scope checker may need old/new path semantics.

deletedFilesPolicy.reason: Deleted files can still matter for forbidden scope, but status-aware handling should be designed separately.

generatedChurnPolicy.reason: Changed-file collection should not hide generated read-model churn. Any suppression or exclusion must be explicit and reviewable.

unresolvedDesignQuestions: How should baseRef and headRef be supplied to a non-enforcing checker command?
Is HEAD~1..HEAD acceptable for the first implementation slice, or should all runs require explicit refs?
Should name-only collection be accepted first, or should name-status be required from the start?
How should rename old/new paths be compared against allowedScope and forbiddenScope?
How should deleted files in forbiddenScope be reported?
What repository case-sensitivity policy should path comparison use?
Which generated churn suppression rules, if any, should be a separate policy layer?
allowedUse: review the intended future git-derived changed-file input model
separate committed-range design from working-tree and staged modes
record path normalization requirements before implementation
prepare a later non-enforcing collection-only artifact
nonExecutionBoundary: This git-derived changed-file input design preview is static and non-enforcing. It does not run git diff, collect changed files, inspect actual diffs, run a checker, evaluate fixture-provided scenarios, normalize paths in code, report no-violation, detect actual violations, reject changes, enforce scope, configure CI or required checks, approve a fixture, satisfy runtime Evidence, set equivalenceProven true, or replace user acceptance.
